title: hoheria lyallii var. glabrata
intro: Hoheria lyallii var. glabrata, also known as the New Zealand lacebark, is a variant of the Hoheria lyallii plant, which is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, providing a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Hoheria lyallii var. glabrata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine.
